title: दत्तात्रेय कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री दत्तात्रेय कवचम् ॥

॥ विनियोगः ॥
अस्य श्री दत्तात्रेय कवचस्य नारद ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्री दत्तात्रेयो देवता।
द्रां बीजम्। श्रीं शक्तिः। क्लीं कीलकम्।
श्री दत्तात्रेयप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
जटाधरं पाण्डुराङ्गं शूलहस्तं कृपानिधिम्।
सर्वरोगहरं देवं दत्तात्रेयमहं भजे॥

॥ कवचम् ॥

दत्तात्रेयः शिरः पातु ललाटं पातु दीनबन्धुः।
भ्रूमध्यं पातु योगीशो नेत्रे पातु दयानिधिः॥१॥

कर्णौ मे पातु सर्वज्ञो नासिकां पातु शंकरः।
मुखं मे पातु सर्वात्मा जिह्वां पातु सुराधिपः॥२॥

कण्ठं मे पातु देवेशो स्कन्धौ पातु कृपामयः।
भुजौ पातु जगन्नाथो हस्तौ पातु शिवात्मकः॥३॥

वक्षः पातु महायोगी हृदयं पातु ईश्वरः।
पार्श्वौ पातु दयासिन्धुरुदरं पातु विश्वधृक्॥४॥

नाभिं मे पातु सिद्धेशो कटिं पातु जगत्प्रभुः।
ऊरू मे पातु सर्वेशो जानुनी पातु वीरभद्रः॥५॥

जंघे मे पातु भूतेशो गुल्फौ पातु परात्परः।
पादौ पातु महासिद्धो सर्वाङ्गं पातु सर्वदा॥६॥

पूर्वतः पातु मां देवः दक्षिणे पातु सर्ववित्।
पश्चिमे पातु योगीशो उत्तरे पातु अत्रिजः॥७॥

ऊर्ध्वं पातु मुनिश्रेष्ठो ह्यधस्तात्पातु भूपतिः।
दिवा पातु परं ज्योतिः रात्रौ पातु परेश्वरः॥८॥

सन्ध्ययोः पातु भगवान् सर्वतः पातु मद्गुरुः।
दत्तात्रेयो मम सदा कवचं भवतु सर्वदा॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
इदं कवचमज्ञात्वा यो भजेद्दत्तमव्ययम्।
न तस्य जायते सिद्धिः कल्पकोटिशतैरपि॥

ज्ञात्वा तु कवचं दिव्यं ज्ञात्वा ध्यानं विधानतः।
सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नोति नात्र कार्या विचारणा॥

सर्वशत्रुक्षयकरं सर्वरोगनिवारणम्।
सर्वग्रहप्रशमनं सर्वमंगलदायकम्॥

॥ इति श्री नारदपुराणोक्तं श्री दत्तात्रेय कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Dattatreya Kavacham ||

|| Viniyogah ||
Asya Shri Dattatreya Kavachasya Narada Rishiph.
Anushtup Chhandah. Shri Dattatreyo Devata.
Dram Bijam. Shrim Shaktih. Klim Kilakam.
Shri Dattatreya-prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

|| Dhyanam ||
Jatadaharam Pandurangam Shula-hastam Kripa-nidhim.
Sarva-roga-haram Devam Dattatreyam-aham Bhaje.

|| Kavacham ||

Dattatreyah Shirah Patu Lalatam Patu Dina-bandhuh.
Bhru-madhyam Patu Yogisho Netre Patu Dayanidhi.||1||

Karnau Me Patu Sarvajno Nasikam Patu Shankarah.
Mukham Me Patu Sarvatma Jihvam Patu Suradhipah.||2||

Kantham Me Patu Devesho Skandhau Patu Kripamayah.
Bhujau Patu Jagannartho Hastau Patu Shivatmakah.||3||

Vakshah Patu Mahayogi Hridayam Patu Ishvarah.
Parshvau Patu Daya-sindhur-udaram Patu Vishva-dhrik.||4||

Nabhim Me Patu Siddhesho Katim Patu Jagat-prabhuh.
Uru Me Patu Sarvesho Januni Patu Virabhadrah.||5||

Janghe Me Patu Bhutesho Gulphau Patu Paratparah.
Padau Patu Mahasiddho Sarvangam Patu Sarvada.||6||

Purvatah Patu Mam Devah Dakshine Patu Sarvavit.
Pashchime Patu Yogisho Uttare Patu Atrijah.||7||

Urdhvam Patu Muni-shreshtho Hy-adhastat-patu Bhupatih.
Diva Patu Param Jyotih Ratrau Patu Pareshvarah.||8||

Sandhyayoh Patu Bhagavan Sarvatah Patu Mad-guruh.
Dattatreyo Mama Sada Kavacham Bhavatu Sarvada.

|| Phalashrutih ||
Idam Kavacham-ajnatva Yo Bhajed-Dattam-avyayam.
Na Tasya Jayate Siddhih Kalpa-koti-shatair-api.

Jnatva Tu Kavacham Divyam Jnatva Dhyanam Vidhanatah.
Sarva-siddhim-avapnoti Natra Karya Vicharana.

Sarva-shatru-kshaya-karam Sarva-roga-nivaranam.
Sarva-graha-prashamanam Sarva-mangala-dayakam.

|| Iti Shri Narada-puranoktam Shri Dattatreya Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
जटाधारी, गौर शरीर वाले, शूल हाथ में धारण करने वाले, कृपा के भण्डार, सभी रोगों को हरने वाले दत्तात्रेय भगवान का मैं भजन करता हूँ।

शिर की दत्तात्रेय, ललाट की दीनबन्धु, भ्रूमध्य की योगीश्वर, नेत्रों की दयानिधि रक्षा करें। कानों की सर्वज्ञ, नासिका की शंकर, मुख की सर्वात्मा, जिह्वा की सुराधिप रक्षा करें।

कण्ठ की देवेश, कन्धों की कृपामय, भुजाओं की जगन्नाथ, हस्तों की शिवात्मक, वक्ष की महायोगी, हृदय की ईश्वर रक्षा करें।

पूर्व में देव, दक्षिण में सर्ववित्, पश्चिम में योगीश, उत्तर में अत्रिज (अत्रि के पुत्र) रक्षा करें। ऊपर मुनिश्रेष्ठ, नीचे भूपति, दिन में परम ज्योति, रात में परेश्वर रक्षा करें।

जो विधिपूर्वक इस कवच को जानकर भजन करता है, वह सर्वसिद्धि प्राप्त करता है। यह कवच शत्रुनाश, रोगनिवारण, ग्रहशमन और सर्वमंगलदायक है।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीदत्तात्रेयकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य नारद ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीदत्तात्रेयो देवता। द्रां बीजम्। श्रीं शक्तिः। क्लीं कीलकम्। श्रीदत्तात्रेयप्रीत्यर्थे दत्तात्रेयकवचजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Provides complete divine armor protection of Lord Dattatreya (Trimurti form)
Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies and removes all diseases
Invokes all siddhis when recited with proper knowledge and dhyana
Invokes the blessing of all-round auspiciousness and protects from all directions
Essential prerequisite before Dattatreya mantra japa
description: Dattatreya Kavacham (दत्तात्रेय कवचम्) is a divine armor prayer for protection dedicated to Lord Dattatreya. This sacred composition is sourced from Narada Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to destroy all enemies and remove all diseases and provide complete divine armor protection of Lord Dattatreya (Trimurti form). It is also said to bestow all-round auspiciousness and protect from all directions. For best results, devotees chant this on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Guru Purnima and Datta Jayanti. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Dattatreya Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Dattatreya Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred armor hymn of Lord Dattatreya for divine protection and spiritual attainment.
relatedSearches: Dattatreya Kavacham lyrics in Sanskrit Marathi Hindi PDF
Dattatreya birth story Atri Anasuya Trimurti
Datta avatara 16 incarnations Sripada Sri Vallabha
Narasimha Saraswati Swami Samarth Sai Baba
Datta mantra Om Drim Dattatreyaya Namaha

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dattatreya Kavacham and what protection does it offer?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dattatreya Kavacham (kavacha = armour) is a Sanskrit protective hymn invoking Lord Dattatreya — the unique Trimurti avatara who embodies the combined essence of Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva in a single form, and who is regarded as the Adi-Guru of the Avadhuta lineage. The kavacham systematically requests Datta to protect each part of the devotee's body (shiras, lalata, netra, karna, mukha, kantha, hridaya, nabhi, kati, janu, pada) and grants protection from disease, debts, enemies, evil spirits and karmic obstructions through the threefold strength of the three gods unified. It is preserved in the Narada Purana and is the central daily recitation of the Datta sampradaya, especially in Maharashtra, Karnataka and Andhra Pradesh. (Sources: Narada Purana; Datta Mahatmya; Hindupedia Dattatreya.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Dattatreya and what is his birth story?
seoFaqs.answer: Dattatreya is the son of Sage Atri (one of the seven sapta-rishis) and his wife Anasuya, born when Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva tested Anasuya's pativrata-dharma by appearing as wandering mendicants demanding she serve them naked. By her power of chastity she turned them into infants and nursed them; pleased, the trinity granted that they be born to her as a single child combining all three. Dattatreya thus has three heads (Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva) and six arms holding the symbols of all three (kamandalu and akshamala of Brahma, shankha and chakra of Vishnu, trishula and damaru of Shiva), accompanied by four dogs (the four Vedas) and a cow (the earth/Kamadhenu). He embodies the unified Brahman across all sectarian forms. (Sources: Markandeya Purana; Datta Mahatmya; Hindupedia Dattatreya.)

seoFaqs.question: Who are the 24 gurus of Dattatreya from the Avadhuta Gita?
seoFaqs.answer: Dattatreya in the Bhagavata Purana (Skandha 11) declares to King Yadu that he learned wisdom from twenty-four gurus drawn from nature: the earth (forbearance), wind (non-attachment), sky (omnipresence), water (purity), fire (austerity), moon (changelessness of the Self despite phases), sun (single light reflected in many vessels), pigeon (warning against attachment to family), python (contentment with whatever comes), ocean (depth and calm), moth (warning against lust for form), bee (taking essence from many flowers without harm), elephant (warning against lust), honey-collector (warning against accumulated wealth), deer (warning against lust for sound), fish (warning against lust for taste), pingala the courtesan (renouncing expectation), kurara bird (renouncing possessions), child (joyful innocence), maiden (working alone), arrow-maker (concentration), serpent (solitary wandering), spider (manifesting and withdrawing the world), and the wasp (becoming what one meditates upon). (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 11.7-9; Avadhuta Gita; Hindupedia Dattatreya.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Dattatreya Kavacham be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The most powerful occasions are Dattatreya Jayanti (Margashirsha Purnima, December full moon — Datta's birth tithi), every Thursday (Guruvar — Datta is Guru), every Margashirsha and Magha month, the Datta Saptaha (week-long parayana of the Gurucharitra culminating on Datta Jayanti), Guru Pournima, and the Datta-Pradosha. Daily recitation in brahma-muhurta (4-6 AM) facing east or north-east, before a Datta image with offerings of red flowers, panchamrita, til-ladoo, and bhakari (the Maharashtra rustic offering), is the prescribed Datta-sampradaya sadhana. Datta is said to walk the earth daily begging alms in disguise, so recitation any time of day is auspicious. (Sources: Drikpanchang Datta Jayanti; Gurucharitra; Hindupedia Datta sampradaya.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the major Datta avataras in the Datta sampradaya?
seoFaqs.answer: The Datta sampradaya recognises a continuous lineage of Datta avataras, the principal sixteen including: Sripada Sri Vallabha (Pithapuram, 14th century, the first historical Datta avatara), Narasimha Saraswati (Ganagapur, 14th-15th century, author of the Gurucharitra), Janardana Swami, Eknath Maharaj, Dasopant, Niranjana Raghunath, Manik Prabhu, Akkalkot Swami Samarth (Akkalkot, 19th century), Vasudevananda Saraswati (Tembe Swami), Sai Baba of Shirdi (regarded as Datta avatara by many lineages), Gajanan Maharaj of Shegaon, and several other 19th-20th century saints across Maharashtra and Karnataka. Reciting the Dattatreya Kavacham invokes all of these avataras as one continuous Datta presence. (Sources: Gurucharitra; Datta Sampradaya tradition; Hindupedia Datta avataras.)

seoFaqs.question: Where are the major Dattatreya temples and Datta-kshetras?
seoFaqs.answer: The principal Datta-kshetras (Datta sacred sites) are mainly in Maharashtra and Karnataka: Ganagapur (Karnataka, the principal seat of Narasimha Saraswati where Datta is said to give darshan to the most-sincere devotees), Audumbar (Maharashtra, site of the audumbar tree where Datta sat), Narasoba-wadi (Maharashtra, where Narasimha Saraswati performed tapasya), Pithapuram (Andhra Pradesh, birth place of Sripada Sri Vallabha), Akkalkot (Maharashtra, site of Swami Samarth), Mahur Garh (Maharashtra, where Datta is said to take his madhukari alms daily), Girnar Mountain (Gujarat, Datta's main meditation seat), and Shirdi (Maharashtra, Sai Baba). The Gurucharitra parayana is the central sadhana at all these kshetras. (Sources: Datta Sampradaya tradition; Maharashtra HRCE; Hindupedia Datta-kshetras.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Dattatreya Kavacham promise to its reciters?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition holds that regular recitation grants the threefold protection of Brahma (creative blessings, knowledge), Vishnu (preservation, prosperity, family welfare) and Shiva (destruction of obstacles, ego, ignorance), removal of all eight forms of karmic obstacles (asta-vighna), recovery from chronic illnesses, removal of debts and progeny obstacles, swift relief from acute crises (since Datta is the Avadhuta who responds instantly), success in spiritual sadhana through guru-grace, freedom from black magic and evil spirits, the cultivation of vairagya and viveka, and ultimately Self-realisation as taught by the twenty-four-fold guru. The Datta sampradaya promises that no one who recites the kavacham daily for one mandala (forty days) goes without Datta's personal darshan in dream or waking. (Sources: Narada Purana phalashruti; Gurucharitra; Hindupedia Datta upasana.)
